scope_statement: Inventory of outcome-relevant environment selectors and trust dependencies in the repository's current GitHub Actions, Python, Docker, native-toolchain, and accepted local verification interfaces. This artifact specifies the current boundary; it does not implement an environment lock.

entries.current_selector: ubuntu-24.04
entries.observed_state: The committed-range whitespace job selects a mutable GitHub-hosted runner label; no runner-image build identity or image digest is recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: The runner supplies the operating system, filesystem behavior, Python bootstrap environment, Git, shell, and service integration used by the check.
evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:18 declares the label and no immutable runner identity.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the resolved runner-image identity and relevant tool versions, or move the evidentiary execution to a content-addressed environment while retaining the hosted-service boundary explicitly.

entries.current_selector: ubuntu-24.04
entries.observed_state: The Python matrix job selects a mutable GitHub-hosted runner label; no runner-image build identity or image digest is recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: The runner determines the platform and native substrate on which interpreter and dependency artifacts are selected and tests execute.
evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:42 declares the label and no immutable runner identity.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the resolved runner-image identity, platform, and relevant preinstalled state for each matrix execution, or use a content-addressed execution environment.

entries.current_selector: ubuntu-24.04
entries.observed_state: The native compiler matrix job selects a mutable GitHub-hosted runner label; no runner-image build identity or image digest is recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: The runner supplies compilers, standard libraries, CMake, Ninja, Make, Git, shell utilities, kernel, and CPU-visible behavior used for builds and smoke checks.
evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:87 declares the label and no immutable runner identity.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the resolved runner image, platform, compiler, standard library, and build-tool identities for any evidence that depends on this job.

entries.current_selector: ubuntu-24.04
entries.observed_state: The non-executing heavy-workflow scaffold selects a mutable GitHub-hosted runner label; no runner-image build identity or image digest is recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: The runner supplies the shell, Python, hashing tools, clock, and artifact-service client used to construct and preserve the scaffold record.
evidence: .github/workflows/heavy-search.yml:50 declares the label and no immutable runner identity.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the resolved runner and tool identities for material workflow records; do not infer a future search environment from this scaffold label.

entries.current_selector: GitHub Actions runner assignment, expression evaluation, contexts, cache, artifact, and orchestration services
entries.observed_state: The repository pins workflow text but does not control or content-address the GitHub Actions control plane, runner protocol, expression evaluator, cache service, artifact service, or service-provided context values.
entries.outcome_relevance: These services decide job placement, expose commit and run identities, transport cached or uploaded bytes, evaluate conditions, and mediate execution.
external_trust_dependencies: GitHub Actions cache and artifact services
evidence: Workflow contexts and service operations are supplied at run time and have no repository content digest.
entries.required_next_action: Define and review the external-service assumptions accepted for each evidence level, and capture service-issued run, runner, cache, and artifact identities where available.

entries.current_selector: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5
entries.observed_state: All checkout occurrences select the accepted actions/checkout v4.3.1 commit. The Action repository source identity is content-pinned; its execution service and Node runtime are separate entries.
entries.outcome_relevance: Checkout determines which repository bytes and history reach later checks.
evidence: The accepted Action-pin dossier verified the official commit object and action.yml at this SHA.
entries.required_next_action: Retain the full commit pin and validator coverage; account separately for source delivery and runtime-service trust.

entries.current_selector: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065
entries.observed_state: All setup-python occurrences select the accepted actions/setup-python v5.6.0 commit. The Action source is content-pinned; interpreter artifacts and the GitHub-supplied Node runtime remain separate.
entries.outcome_relevance: The Action resolves, obtains, installs, exposes, and optionally caches Python interpreter and package artifacts.
evidence: The accepted Action-pin dossier verified the official commit object and action.yml at this SHA.
entries.required_next_action: Retain the full commit pin and validator coverage; lock or capture interpreter resolution and runtime-service inputs separately.

entries.current_selector: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02
entries.observed_state: All upload-artifact occurrences select the accepted actions/upload-artifact v4.6.2 commit. The Action source is content-pinned; artifact transport/storage and the Node runtime remain external.
entries.outcome_relevance: The Action selects and transfers failure and scaffold evidence to an external storage service.
evidence: The accepted Action-pin dossier verified the official commit object and action.yml at this SHA.
entries.required_next_action: Retain the full commit pin and verify uploaded artifact hashes independently of service transport and retention.

entries.current_selector: runs.using: node20 in each of the three pinned Action metadata files; executable runtime supplied by GitHub
entries.observed_state: Official action.yml at each accepted Action commit declares node20. The pinned metadata fixes that major-runtime request, but the Node executable build and bytes are supplied by the GitHub runner service and are not recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: The supplied Node runtime executes all pinned JavaScript Action code before and around repository commands.
evidence: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/actions/checkout/34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5/action.yml declares runs.using node20.
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/actions/setup-python/a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065/action.yml declares runs.using node20.
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/actions/upload-artifact/ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02/action.yml declares runs.using node20.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the runner-provided Node identity where available and explicitly accept or avoid this runtime trust dependency for V3 and V4 evidence.

entries.observed_state: The setup-python input fixes the CPython major/minor family but permits patch, build, platform artifact, and acquisition details to be resolved at run time.
entries.outcome_relevance: Interpreter patch level, build configuration, ABI, standard library, and installer contents can affect dependency selection and test behavior.
evidence: The workflow records only the 3.11 selector; no interpreter artifact digest is present.
entries.required_next_action: Choose and verify an exact interpreter distribution and artifact identity, or capture the resolved patch/build/artifact identity for every material run.

entries.observed_state: The Python matrix fixes the CPython major/minor family but permits patch, build, platform artifact, and acquisition details to be resolved at run time.
entries.outcome_relevance: Interpreter patch level, build configuration, ABI, standard library, and installer contents can affect dependency selection and test behavior.
evidence: The workflow records only the 3.12 selector; no interpreter artifact digest is present.
entries.required_next_action: Choose and verify an exact interpreter distribution and artifact identity, or capture the resolved patch/build/artifact identity for every material run.

entries.current_selector: setup-python resolution and runner tool-cache/download artifacts for declared 3.11 and 3.12 selectors; architecture input omitted
entries.observed_state: Exact interpreter patch versions, build identities, architecture choice, tool-cache hits, downloaded archive identities, and hashes are not repository-recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: The chosen distribution determines the executable, ABI, standard library, bundled installer, supported wheels, and native extension compatibility.
external_trust_dependencies: GitHub runner architecture and tool cache
evidence: The setup-python architecture input is absent and no interpreter archive digest or build identity is stored.
entries.required_next_action: Record exact resolved versions, architecture, executable identity, distribution source, and archive hashes, with an independently checkable acquisition route.

entries.current_selector: gcc and g++ resolved from the ubuntu-24.04 runner PATH
entries.observed_state: Compiler names are committed, but exact GCC, binutils, target, libstdc++, libc, header, and runtime versions are supplied by the mutable runner image and are not captured.
entries.outcome_relevance: Compiler and standard-library implementation choices can change diagnostics, generated code, ABI behavior, optimization, and process results.
evidence: The workflow chooses bare gcc/g++ names and records no version or executable hash.
entries.required_next_action: Capture compiler target, version, executable hash, standard-library and linker identities, or execute inside a content-addressed native toolchain environment.

entries.current_selector: clang and clang++ resolved from the ubuntu-24.04 runner PATH
entries.observed_state: Compiler names are committed, but exact Clang, linker, target, C++ standard-library, libc, header, and runtime versions are supplied by the mutable runner image and are not captured.
entries.outcome_relevance: Compiler and standard-library implementation choices can change diagnostics, generated code, ABI behavior, optimization, and process results.
evidence: The workflow chooses bare clang/clang++ names and records no version or executable hash.
entries.required_next_action: Capture compiler target, version, executable hash, standard-library and linker identities, or execute inside a content-addressed native toolchain environment.

entries.current_selector: cmake resolved from the ubuntu-24.04 runner PATH
entries.observed_state: The workflow uses the hosted CMake executable without an exact version or artifact identity.
entries.outcome_relevance: CMake version and policy behavior determine compiler discovery, generated build rules, flags, and dependency metadata.
evidence: The workflow invokes bare cmake and records no version or executable hash.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the exact CMake version and executable identity for material builds, or supply it from a content-addressed environment.

entries.current_selector: Ninja generator and ninja executable resolved from the ubuntu-24.04 runner PATH
entries.observed_state: The generator name is committed, but the hosted Ninja version and executable identity are not captured.
entries.outcome_relevance: Ninja interprets generated dependency and scheduling rules and controls the parallel native build.
evidence: The sources select Ninja by name and record no version or executable hash.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the exact Ninja version and executable identity for material builds, or supply it from a content-addressed environment.

entries.current_selector: make resolved from the ubuntu-24.04 runner PATH by the integration test
entries.observed_state: The integration route requires Make, but the hosted GNU Make version and executable identity are not captured.
entries.outcome_relevance: Make interprets the preserved upstream build graph, variables, wildcard expansion, shell commands, and compiler invocation.
evidence: The integration test resolves make by name when EG_MAKE is absent.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the Make version, executable identity, and invoked shell/tool identities for material builds.

entries.current_selector: git resolved from the ubuntu-24.04 runner PATH and used by checkout and repository checks
entries.observed_state: The hosted Git version, build, executable identity, effective configuration, and external attributes inputs are not fully captured by the workflow.
entries.outcome_relevance: Git selects repository objects, applies attributes and configuration, reports worktree state, and evaluates whitespace.
evidence: Workflow commands invoke bare git and record no version or executable hash.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the Git version, executable identity, and effective configuration/attributes boundary for material repository-state evidence.

entries.current_selector: hosted bash/default shell plus test, grep, date, sha256sum, awk, mkdir, printf, echo, and related core utilities
entries.observed_state: Utility names and shell scripts are committed, but the shell/coreutils implementations, versions, locale behavior, and executable identities come from the mutable runner image.
entries.outcome_relevance: These tools implement guards, hashing, timestamps, diagnostics, file creation, filtering, and status checks.
external_trust_dependencies: GitHub-hosted runner shell and core utilities
evidence: The workflows invoke hosted shell utilities by name without version or artifact capture.
entries.required_next_action: Capture shell and core-utility versions, locale, timezone, and executable identities when their output is material evidence.

entries.current_selector: platform and resources implied by the GitHub ubuntu-24.04 hosted label and omitted setup-python architecture input
entries.observed_state: CPU architecture/features/count, kernel, libc, memory, filesystem, locale, timezone, and runner-image build are not selected or captured exactly.
entries.outcome_relevance: Platform and resource details affect artifact selection, ABI, native code, concurrency, timing, memory behavior, and path semantics.
external_trust_dependencies: GitHub-hosted runner platform and resource assignment
evidence: No workflow architecture, CPU feature, kernel, libc, memory, locale, or timezone capture is present.
entries.required_next_action: Capture all outcome-relevant platform and resource fields for material executions and define which externally assigned properties are accepted in the trust boundary.

entries.current_selector: CC/CXX matrix names, EG_CANDIDATE_INSPECTION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, EG_SURPRISING_OUTCOME_DIR, GitHub context variables, GITHUB_ENV, inherited PATH, locale, timezone, and process environment
entries.observed_state: Several committed variables are explicit, while GitHub contexts and the inherited process environment are resolved externally; no complete effective environment capture is emitted.
entries.outcome_relevance: Environment variables select tools, timeouts, artifact paths, run identities, locale and path behavior, and may influence Python, Git, compiler, and hashing behavior.
external_trust_dependencies: GitHub Actions context and process-environment injection
evidence: The workflows declare selected variables but do not serialize a reviewed allowlist of all effective execution-affecting variables.
entries.required_next_action: Define an environment-variable allowlist and capture effective values relevant to tool discovery, hashing, scheduling, locale, time, and execution for material runs.

entries.observed_state: The project declares only a lower compatibility bound; it does not select one interpreter patch, build, ABI, or distribution.
entries.outcome_relevance: Different supported Python implementations and versions can select different dependencies and exhibit different interpreter behavior.
evidence: pyproject.toml:10 contains a lower bound rather than an exact distribution identity.
entries.required_next_action: For each material run, select and verify an exact supported interpreter distribution while retaining the compatibility declaration separately.

entries.current_selector: setuptools.build_meta backed by the direct setuptools==82.0.0 requirement
entries.observed_state: The backend name and direct distribution version are selected, but backend wheel/sdist bytes, transitive build requirements, and installer environment are not hash-locked.
entries.outcome_relevance: The backend interprets project metadata and constructs editable-install artifacts and import paths.
evidence: pyproject.toml names setuptools.build_meta and separately pins setuptools 82.0.0 by version.
entries.required_next_action: Hash-lock the selected backend distribution and any build requirements, and record the installer and build-isolation semantics.

entries.current_selector: setuptools==82.0.0
entries.observed_state: The direct build requirement version is exact, but no wheel/sdist hash or index artifact identity is recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: Setuptools implements project build and editable-install behavior.
external_trust_dependencies: Python package index and installer artifact delivery
evidence: pyproject.toml:2 pins only the distribution version.
entries.required_next_action: Record approved distribution artifact hashes and the acquisition index/source for this exact version.

entries.current_selector: jsonschema==4.26.0
entries.observed_state: The direct runtime requirement version is exact, but no wheel/sdist hash or index artifact identity is recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: jsonschema and its transitive dependencies validate every project JSON Schema and instance used by tooling and tests.
external_trust_dependencies: Python package index and installer artifact delivery
evidence: pyproject.toml:13 pins only the distribution version.
entries.required_next_action: Record approved distribution artifact hashes, transitive lock data, and the acquisition index/source for this exact version.

entries.current_selector: pytest==9.0.2
entries.observed_state: The direct test requirement version is exact, but no wheel/sdist hash or index artifact identity is recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: pytest controls test discovery, fixtures, markers, assertion handling, subprocess test orchestration, and reported results.
external_trust_dependencies: Python package index and installer artifact delivery
evidence: pyproject.toml:18 pins only the distribution version.
entries.required_next_action: Record approved distribution artifact hashes, transitive lock data, and the acquisition index/source for this exact version.

entries.current_selector: python -m pip supplied by each selected interpreter environment
entries.observed_state: The pip executable version, build, configuration, index selection, trust configuration, and bootstrap provenance are not pinned or captured by repository sources.
entries.outcome_relevance: pip resolves candidates, evaluates markers, chooses wheels or source distributions, performs builds, installs dependencies, and may use caches.
external_trust_dependencies: Python package index and artifact hosts
evidence: All install routes invoke the interpreter's unversioned pip module.
entries.required_next_action: Select and record the installer identity, configuration, index sources, build-isolation behavior, and executable or distribution artifact hash.

entries.current_selector: transitive dependency resolution induced by setuptools==82.0.0, jsonschema==4.26.0, and pytest==9.0.2
entries.observed_state: No transitive lock records exact distribution versions, dependency markers, platform choices, or complete resolution output.
entries.outcome_relevance: Transitive packages implement schema formats, reference handling, attributes, packaging, plugins, and other behavior exercised by project tools and tests.
external_trust_dependencies: Python package index metadata and resolver inputs
evidence: pyproject.toml declares only direct dependencies and research/REPRODUCIBILITY.md explicitly records the absent transitive lock.
entries.required_next_action: Produce a reviewed cross-platform or environment-specific transitive lock with exact versions, markers, sources, and resolution semantics.

entries.current_selector: resolver-selected wheels or source distributions, with workflow pip caches enabled and Docker cache disabled
entries.observed_state: No hashes identify direct or transitive wheel/sdist bytes, build outputs, cache contents, or platform-specific artifact choices.
entries.outcome_relevance: Different artifacts for the same version can carry different code, metadata, native extensions, build results, and supply-chain provenance.
evidence: Direct version pins have no accompanying hashes, and cache policy differs between workflow and Docker routes.
entries.required_next_action: Hash-lock every accepted direct and transitive artifact for each supported platform and record cache provenance or disable unverified cache inputs.

entries.current_selector: pip default and environment-configured package indexes and artifact hosts; no repository index URL is declared
entries.observed_state: Package metadata and artifacts are acquired through external services selected by pip defaults or process configuration, without a repository-recorded service snapshot or attestation.
entries.outcome_relevance: Service metadata, availability, yanks, uploads, and artifact delivery influence dependency resolution and installed bytes.
evidence: No --index-url, snapshot identity, or artifact hash set appears in any install route.
entries.required_next_action: Record approved index/source identities and require independent artifact hashes so service state cannot silently change accepted bytes.

entries.current_selector: CMake minimum 3.25, represented as 3.25 and 3.25.0 lower bounds
entries.observed_state: The repository sets a minimum compatible CMake version rather than an exact CMake binary or artifact.
entries.outcome_relevance: Later CMake releases may change policies, compiler discovery, generated rules, defaults, and diagnostics while satisfying the bound.
evidence: CMakeLists.txt and CMakePresets.json encode only the 3.25 lower bound.
entries.required_next_action: Select and capture an exact CMake binary identity for each evidentiary build while retaining the source compatibility minimum separately.

entries.current_selector: C++17 without extensions; Ninja; Debug or Release CMake presets; Release in CI/Docker/local integration; warning flags; upstream Make -O3
entries.observed_state: These repository-controlled build selections are fixed by the inspected commit. Actual compiler-expanded flags, platform defaults, tool versions, and runtime libraries remain in separate unresolved entries.
entries.outcome_relevance: Language mode, optimization, warnings, generator, target, and build type determine compilation semantics and generated executable behavior.
evidence: The inspected commit fixes the listed source-level build configuration values.
entries.required_next_action: Retain these committed selectors and record compiler-expanded commands and flags together with the separately locked or captured toolchain.

entries.current_selector: ubuntu:24.04@sha256:4fbb8e6a8395de5a7550b33509421a2bafbc0aab6c06ba2cef9ebffbc7092d90
entries.observed_state: The Ubuntu multi-platform image index is selected by a full sha256 digest. Platform-child selection and later APT resolution are separate unresolved entries.
entries.outcome_relevance: The index fixes the set of platform manifests from which the base filesystem, package-source configuration, and initial tools are selected.
external_trust_dependencies: OCI registry availability and digest-verified delivery
evidence: Dockerfile:1 contains a full sha256 digest and no mutable tag-only selector.
entries.required_next_action: Retain the index digest and record the selected platform child manifest and verified base-image acquisition details for every material build.

entries.current_selector: implicit builder/default platform; Dockerfile has no --platform selector and records no selected child-manifest digest
entries.observed_state: The index is immutable, but the host/build platform selects one child manifest and package architecture. That platform and child digest are not repository-fixed or captured.
entries.outcome_relevance: The selected architecture changes base filesystem bytes, package candidates, Python ABI, compiler target, native code, and runtime behavior.
evidence: Dockerfile:1 omits --platform and no child manifest is recorded elsewhere.
entries.required_next_action: Select or capture the exact OS/architecture platform and resolved child-manifest digest for every built image.

entries.current_selector: external Docker/BuildKit engine, frontend, snapshotter, runtime, and build platform; no repository version selector
entries.observed_state: The image was not built in the recorded local baseline, so engine version, frontend behavior, platform, daemon configuration, and image output identity are unavailable.
entries.outcome_relevance: Builder implementation controls Dockerfile interpretation, platform resolution, cache behavior, timestamps/metadata, filesystem layers, networking, and final image bytes.
evidence: research/REPRODUCIBILITY.md explicitly records that Docker was unavailable and the image was not built locally.
entries.required_next_action: Record builder/frontend/runtime versions, configuration, build platform, command, cache policy, and resulting image digest when the container route is exercised.

entries.current_selector: COPY of the local dot context filtered by .dockerignore
entries.observed_state: The Dockerfile copies all nonignored context bytes. A clean commit, untracked-file inventory, canonical context archive, and context digest are not enforced by this route.
entries.outcome_relevance: Dirty or untracked nonignored files can change installed project code, tests, metadata, build inputs, and image bytes without changing the inspected commit.
evidence: Dockerfile:23 copies the dot context and .dockerignore excludes local build state but does not assert a canonical committed-only archive.
entries.required_next_action: Build from a verified canonical context and record its sorted file inventory and digest together with clean-tree status.

entries.current_selector: APT sources inherited from the selected base child and mutable apt-get update index state at build time
entries.observed_state: No Ubuntu snapshot timestamp, Release-file digest, repository identity inventory, or saved package index is repository-controlled or captured.
entries.outcome_relevance: Index state determines which package versions, architectures, dependencies, and download artifacts satisfy every unversioned package name.
external_trust_dependencies: Ubuntu APT repositories and signing infrastructure
evidence: Dockerfile runs apt-get update against base-configured sources without a snapshot selector.
entries.required_next_action: Record and verify exact APT source configuration, signed index identities, snapshot time or equivalent immutable index, and package download metadata.

entries.current_selector: Ubuntu archive endpoints and trust roots inherited from the base image
entries.observed_state: Package indexes and archives are obtained from an external service whose availability, retained versions, mirrors, and delivery are not repository-controlled.
entries.outcome_relevance: The service supplies index metadata and package archives used to create the entire container toolchain and Python environment.
external_trust_dependencies: Ubuntu archive, mirrors, and signing keys
evidence: The Dockerfile comment records that the public archive does not retain every superseded package.
entries.required_next_action: Define the accepted repository/signing trust boundary and independently verify content hashes even when an immutable snapshot service is used.

entries.current_selector: build-essential
entries.observed_state: The package name is unversioned; the meta-package version and dependency closure are resolved from the mutable APT index.
entries.outcome_relevance: This selector introduces the container compiler, standard library development files, linker, Make, headers, and related native build dependencies.
external_trust_dependencies: Ubuntu APT metadata and package archives
evidence: Dockerfile:11 contains no package version constraint.
entries.required_next_action: Record the exact resolved meta-package and full dependency closure with versions, architectures, repository identities, and archive hashes.

entries.current_selector: ca-certificates
entries.observed_state: The package name is unversioned; certificate bundle version and archive bytes are resolved from the mutable APT index.
entries.outcome_relevance: The trust store authenticates HTTPS acquisition of Python and other external artifacts inside the container.
external_trust_dependencies: Ubuntu APT metadata and package archives
evidence: Dockerfile:12 contains no package version constraint.
entries.required_next_action: Record the exact package version, architecture, archive hash, repository identity, and resulting trust-store identity.

entries.current_selector: cmake
entries.observed_state: The package name is unversioned; CMake version and archive bytes are resolved from the mutable APT index.
entries.outcome_relevance: CMake configures the native build and produces build rules and compiler invocations.
external_trust_dependencies: Ubuntu APT metadata and package archives
evidence: Dockerfile:13 contains no package version constraint.

external_trust_dependencies: Ubuntu APT metadata and package archives

entries.current_selector: APT-resolved compiler, binutils, libc/libstdc++ development and runtime components, CMake, Ninja, Make, Git, bash, and core utilities
entries.observed_state: Exact compiler target/version, standard-library and libc versions, linker, build tools, shell utilities, executable hashes, and compiler-expanded flags are unavailable until a recorded image build resolves the unversioned package set.
entries.outcome_relevance: This closure compiles, links, launches, hashes, and tests the preserved native implementation inside the container.
external_trust_dependencies: Ubuntu package closure and container runtime
evidence: The Dockerfile names unversioned meta-packages/tools and records no resolved package or executable inventory.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the full installed package closure, compiler target and standard libraries, tool versions/hashes, expanded compile/link commands, and resulting executable hash.

entries.observed_state: CPython patch/build/ABI, executable hash, standard library, pip/ensurepip identity, virtual-environment contents, and artifact provenance are not recorded.
entries.outcome_relevance: The runtime selects Python behavior and package artifacts and executes every verifier, schema, test, and build orchestration command in the image.
evidence: The Docker route has only unversioned APT package names and a fixed virtual-environment path.
entries.required_next_action: Capture exact interpreter, ABI, executable, standard-library, pip, venv, package, and artifact identities from a reproducible image build.

entries.current_selector: python -m pip install --no-cache-dir -e ".[test]"
entries.observed_state: The route disables pip's download cache but performs live editable resolution without a lockfile, artifact hashes, explicit index, or captured resolution report.
entries.outcome_relevance: The command resolves and installs the backend, direct, and transitive distributions that execute all container verification.
external_trust_dependencies: Python package indexes and artifact hosts
evidence: Dockerfile:30 contains the exact live install route and no hash/lock arguments.
entries.required_next_action: Replace or constrain live resolution with a reviewed exact dependency/artifact set and verify the installed environment against it.

entries.current_selector: DEBIAN_FRONTEND default, overridable RESEARCH_UID=10001, researcher user, /bin/bash, fixed VIRTUAL_ENV prepend, inherited base PATH and other process variables
entries.observed_state: Some defaults and paths are committed, but build arguments may override values and PATH, locale, timezone, proxy, package-index, compiler, and runtime variables inherit from the base/builder environment without a complete capture.
entries.outcome_relevance: User identity, shell, PATH and environment select executables, acquisition endpoints, locale/time behavior, writable locations, and build/test semantics.
evidence: Dockerfile fixes selected defaults but expands inherited PATH and accepts overridable build arguments.
entries.required_next_action: Record effective build arguments and an allowlisted effective environment, including PATH resolution, locale, timezone, proxies, package indexes, compiler variables, and hashing/scheduling inputs.

entries.current_selector: bare local python/venv/pip route; bootstrap observed Python 3.14.3
entries.observed_state: A prior local Python version is documented, but the interpreter distribution/build, executable hash, standard library, pip identity, virtual environment, installed artifacts, and current PATH selection are not repository-locked.
entries.outcome_relevance: The selected local Python executes every required validator and test and selects platform-specific dependencies.
external_trust_dependencies: Locally installed Python distribution and package environment
evidence: research/REPRODUCIBILITY.md records the bootstrap version and explicitly records missing interpreter and transitive artifact locks.
entries.required_next_action: Capture exact interpreter/build/executable, standard library, pip, installed distribution/artifact, PATH, and virtual-environment identities for each local evidentiary run.

entries.current_selector: override directories prepended to inherited PATH; Make receives /ucrt64/bin:/usr/bin; matching MinGW/UCRT runtime DLL and MSYS2 Unix utility closure
entries.observed_state: The accepted command named a PATH prefix, while the inherited suffix, DLL search results, MSYS2 bash/coreutils, mkdir/rm, UCRT/MinGW runtime, locale, and package/artifact provenance are not captured.
entries.outcome_relevance: PATH and DLL resolution select the actual compiler helpers, runtime libraries, shell, Make subcommands, and Unix utilities used by local builds and processes.
external_trust_dependencies: Local Windows loader and MSYS2 runtime/tool installation
evidence: The integration test deliberately augments inherited PATH from override directories and hard-codes an MSYS2 Make PATH.
entries.required_next_action: Capture the complete effective PATH, resolved executable/DLL graph, shell/coreutils versions and hashes, locale, and MSYS2 package/acquisition provenance.

entries.current_selector: bare local Git plus effective system/global/local/worktree configuration, attributes, and filesystem repository metadata
entries.observed_state: Git 2.45.1 behavior is documented for one accepted check and the whitespace helper neutralizes many inputs, but the local executable artifact/provenance and all external Git configuration/filesystem state are not repository-locked.
entries.outcome_relevance: Git resolves commits and ancestry, enumerates scope, applies attributes/configuration, normalizes bytes, and supplies whitespace and preservation evidence.
external_trust_dependencies: Host filesystem and effective Git configuration sources
evidence: research/REPRODUCIBILITY.md records both the installed Git behavior and a rejected archive affected by core.autocrlf.
entries.required_next_action: Capture Git executable/build/hash and effective configuration/attributes inputs for material checks, preserving the helper's explicit neutralization and fail-closed boundary.

entries.current_selector: implicit local Windows/MSYS2 host, UCRT64 path family, CPU architecture/features/resources, memory, filesystem, locale, timezone, and process environment
entries.observed_state: The accepted local verification paths imply a Windows/MSYS2 route, but OS build, CPU architecture/features/count, UCRT/MinGW ABI, memory, filesystem semantics, locale, timezone, and full process environment are not repository-captured.
entries.outcome_relevance: The platform determines executable format, ABI, runtime libraries, path handling, subprocess behavior, artifact selection, scheduling, memory, timing, and locale-sensitive operations.
external_trust_dependencies: Local Windows operating system, hardware, filesystem, and process environment
evidence: The prior accepted dossier records paths and a successful suite, not a complete host/platform attestation.
entries.required_next_action: Capture OS build, architecture, CPU features/count, memory limits, ABI/runtime, filesystem, locale/timezone, PATH, and execution-affecting environment variables for material local runs.

limitations: This inventory describes selectors present at inspected commit 41fb0a9b64ff2c6deeeb8080f41d1ea82bcb568d; it does not implement any lock or attest a later execution.

Hosted GitHub Actions and the container image were not executed by this acquisition task, so runner-image identities, interpreter artifacts, package resolutions, and container output digests are not invented here.
Documented local versions and paths are bounded observations, not accepted package, installer, dependency, or artifact-hash provenance.
CAPTURE_ONLY and NOT_REPOSITORY_CONTROLLED entries require explicit trust-boundary treatment even when a future run records their observed values.

The blocker flags describe evidence produced through the listed interface: unresolved entries do not prevent bounded V1 engineering checks, but they prevent treating the current environment record as sufficient for V3 reproduction or V4 certification.
No entry changes a mathematical claim, pruning status, search state, reproduction status, or certification status.
